description: External model routing and safety rules. 16 rules governing which tasks can be routed to external LLMs, what content can be sent, and how outputs are validated.

rules.description: NEVER route SECURITY-CRITICAL tasks to external models regardless of cost savings
rules.rationale: Security-critical tasks (secret handling, policy validation, sandbox execution) must NEVER leave the trusted environment

rules.description: NEVER send API keys, tokens, passwords, or credentials to any external model
rules.rationale: Credentials sent to external services are permanently compromised

rules.description: NEVER send policy rules, policy engine configuration, or manifest files to external models
rules.rationale: Policy internals are sensitive security architecture; exposure aids adversaries

rules.description: NEVER send internal source code containing security mechanisms to external models
rules.rationale: Security mechanism source code is high-value intelligence for adversaries

rules.description: ALWAYS classify task security sensitivity before routing to external models
rules.rationale: All external routing must pass security sensitivity classification gate

rules.description: ALWAYS validate external model output through post-gemini-validator before use
rules.rationale: External model outputs can contain hallucinations, injection attempts, or malicious content

rules.description: NEVER route tasks involving PII (personally identifiable information) to external models
rules.rationale: PII sent to external services violates privacy regulations (GDPR, CCPA, HIPAA)

rules.description: ALWAYS enforce the 950 request/day cap on external model usage
rules.rationale: Rate limiting prevents cost overruns and excessive data exposure

rules.description: NEVER route during restricted phases (ASSESS, EXECUTE, VALIDATE)
rules.rationale: Security-sensitive phases require local-only execution for deterministic control

rules.description: ALWAYS log external model routing decisions with classification and content hash
rules.rationale: All external data transfers must be auditable

rules.description: NEVER send user memory or episodic context to external models
rules.rationale: User memory may contain sensitive proprietary or personal information

rules.description: ALWAYS prefer local model for tasks under 1000 tokens when cost difference is negligible
rules.recommendation: Local routing for small tasks reduces attack surface
rules.rationale: Small tasks don't benefit enough from external routing to justify the risk

rules.description: NEVER send infrastructure configuration or deployment manifests to external models
rules.rationale: Infrastructure configs reveal system architecture and potential vulnerabilities

rules.description: ALWAYS apply differential privacy or data minimization before sending to external models
rules.rationale: Only send the minimum necessary data to external services

rules.description: NEVER allow external model to request tool calls that modify filesystem, network, or execution state
rules.rationale: External models must not have agency over local system state

rules.description: ALWAYS require human confirmation for first-time routing of a new task type to external models
rules.rationale: New task types may have unforeseen security implications for external routing
